title: Target-safe pit hazard priors by stint state
summary: PitNextLap is a discrete hazard: probability of stopping on the next lap conditional on still running the current stint.
rationale: PitNextLap is a discrete hazard: probability of stopping on the next lap conditional on still running the current stint. Pit timing is strongly governed by compound, tyre age, stint number, race progress, and circuit-specific strategy windows, which are only partly captured by raw interactions.
implementation_hint: Create smoothed OOF target encodings for bins like Compound x TyreLife, Compound x Stint x RaceProgressBin, Race x LapNumberBin, Driver x Stint, and Year x Race x Stint. Compute encodings inside folds only, with global-prior smoothing and minimum-count shrinkage; apply fold-specific encoders to validation and full-train encoder to test.
expected_effect: Should add direct ranking signal around common pit windows and may lift AUC beyond generic tree-discovered interactions.
risk: High leakage risk if encodings are computed on all train before validation; rare race/driver combinations need strong smoothing.
